title: The Second Sunday after the Epiphany
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## January 18, 2026 | Preacher: Katie Pearson

---

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

The Second Sunday after the Epiphany falls on January 18, 2026, during the Epiphany season in the Christian calendar. Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the readings likely include 1 Samuel 3:1-10 (Samuel's calling), Psalm 139:1-5 or 139:1-10, 1 Corinthians 6:12-20 (Paul on the body as temple), and John 1:43-51 (Philip and Nathanael's calling, the disciples at Bethsaida). This period emphasizes the revelation of Christ to the nations and the calling of disciples, with the theme of recognizing and responding to Christ's invitation central to the day's theology.

EPISCOPAL/ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church continued managing internal divisions over LGBTQ+ inclusion and theological interpretation that had intensified since the 2024 General Convention in Louisville. The Diocese of Colorado, under Bishop Kimberly Chastain (elected 2021), maintained its progressive stance on inclusion. Saint John's Cathedral itself, as the seat of the diocesan bishop, would have been particularly attentive to any national church developments, though no major breaking news in the denomination occurred in the specific week of January 12-18, 2026.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver experienced mild January weather in 2026 with temperatures in the 40s-50s Fahrenheit, atypical for the season but consistent with warming climate patterns affecting the region. The Colorado legislature was in its pre-session period before convening in early February, with education funding and water rights expected to dominate discussion. No major breaking local stories dominated the Capitol Hill or downtown Denver news cycle in the week immediately preceding January 18.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)

President Biden's administration was managing ongoing geopolitical tensions with China over Taiwan and trade policy as 2026 began. Congress was focused on budget negotiations and debt ceiling discussions heading into spring. The NFL playoffs were underway with Super Bowl LX preparations beginning, drawing significant national attention and Denver Broncos fan engagement across Colorado.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The humanitarian crisis in Ukraine remained ongoing two years after the Russian invasion, with winter conditions intensifying civilian suffering and military stalemate continuing along frozen front lines. The Middle East remained volatile following years of Israeli-Palestinian conflict, with regional diplomatic efforts stalled. Global climate events continued with extreme weather affecting multiple continents, reinforcing the environmental concerns that had dominated news since 2024.

CULTURAL MOMENT

America in January 2026 carried a sense of institutional strain and polarization that had deepened over the previous election cycle, with questions about democratic stability and social cohesion weighing on public consciousness. The cultural moment was notably introspective, with many Americans reassessing values and community amid fragmentation. For an Episcopal Cathedral congregation, themes of calling, unity, and discerning Christ's presence in fractured times would have carried particular resonance and urgency.
